summary: The coder's in-memory datasource implementation is clean, focused, and OBP-compliant. The sentinel pattern is correct, the Building object follows established Test/Debug conventions, and Actor.CreateDataSource navigates engine state properly. All 29 datasource tests pass including 7 new in-memory tests that verify intent through side-effect assertions (checking disk artifacts rather than just return values). However, the branch diverged from runtime2 before the SettingsData sharing fix (af3b34a9), meaning %Settings.X% is unreachable from User/Service context on this branch. This is a merge gap, not a coder regression, but it must be resolved during merge. One minor carry-forward finding from the security report (DeserializeValue exception gap) remains.

findings.issue: Merge gap: SettingsData registered only on System actor's MemoryStack. runtime2 was fixed (af3b34a9) to share SettingsData across all actors so %Settings.X% resolves from any context. This branch diverged before that fix. User/Service actors cannot resolve %Settings.ApiKey% — MemoryStack.Get returns null.
findings.impact: Any PLang goal using %Settings.X% will fail because goals run in User context by default (Engine.Context => User.Context). This is the most common settings access pattern.
findings.suggestion: When merging to runtime2, preserve the shared SettingsData pattern from runtime2: keep engine.SettingsVariable on Engine, register it on ALL actors via Context.MemoryStack.Put(engine.SettingsVariable). Combine with the in-memory check in CreateDataSource().

findings.issue: Carry-forward: DeserializeValue catches JsonException but not InvalidOperationException from Data.UnwrapJsonElement depth guard. Get/GetAll only catch SqliteException | IOException.
findings.impact: Currently unreachable (JsonDocument.Parse MaxDepth=64 catches first). Violates 'behavior methods never throw' contract. Would become reachable if JsonDocumentOptions.MaxDepth were ever increased.
findings.suggestion: Add catch (InvalidOperationException) in DeserializeValue, return json as raw string. Defensive fix that protects against future changes.

findings.issue: Private constructor parameter 'bool inMemory' is never read — exists only to disambiguate from the public constructor signature.
findings.impact: None. Works correctly. Minor readability concern — a reader might expect the parameter to control behavior.
findings.suggestion: Could use a private tag type (e.g., private enum Mode { InMemory }) for clearer intent. Not worth changing.
